item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could separate the mime and make it a -J, it could work well like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Silly science question: how come the mime doesn't fall through the floor and end up stuck at the Earth's core? I mean… why won't he. Go on son. Phase through the ground and out of our misery, and take your spider generator with you.

edit: also how the heck did you manage to make a footnote without the footnote?! Is Site-5 really that antimemetic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god… it's already got a mime, who's also a ghost, and also hundreds and hundreds of spiders. Why you gotta bring clowns into it as well?

I mean, we're already frustrated by not being able to kill the mime with fire, and there being a potentially infinite number of spiders that would overwhelm you / exhaust your fuel supply if you tried to kill those with fire, so shit's already fucked. It doesn't need that kind of help.

Lucky that the test logs made me lol, really, saving it from becoming a purely horrific dead loss.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
It's more that Safe means 'well-understood and finalized containment procedure', even if that procedure requires regular feeding, medical evaluation, and re-welding of the door every three days, or similar.

Euclids we aren't quite sure we've figured out, but we have them and they're contained/containable for now.

Keter anomalies are poorly understood, poorly contained, and inherently hazardous.

It's like that, I think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And then angry spiders decided to be it

+1

item.title: Das Uberspider
item.content:encoded: …so, my first ever attempt at adding to a collaborative testing log. I hope it passes muster. :0

item.title: Re: A Magic Mime and his Mighty Morphin' Method Acting Spider Kids
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, Shaggy.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
Agreed, and those should only be Safe if they are also physically or mentally unable to attempt escape.

Personally I think that one of the obvious in-universe uses for object classes would be to enable category priority decisions in situations like budgeting, staffing, and abandonment procedures. This only works if Safe universally means no routine maintenance is required for continued containment.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the discussion on this. It's my belief that (as I subscribe to the locked box test) any humanoid should get Euclid so long as they require resources to maintain.

So the exception, at least for me, would be humanoids like robots that don't need maintenance, or those found in anomalous media, like paintings or recordings or text.

While Shaggy is right, it still seems the overwhelming amount of humanoids are Euclid or Keter.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
Safe humanoids are generally ones that have no ability to escape or are willingly contained such as with 507.

The problem is that most of those are also biological, and therefore would die if you locked them in a box and forgot about them.

507 being extremely difficult to actually contain probably wouldn't be Safe if written now, anyway, even if you don't agree that needing routine maintenance means Euclid.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
I think the lock box test didn't exist for most or all of Series I.

Certainly if you subscribe to it, nothing living can be Safe. Obviously you can write successful things that violate locked box anyway.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded: It's more than possible for a humanoid to be Safe. It's just suggested they be at least Euclid, as there's the possibility they may try to escape or work against the Foundation. Safe humanoids are generally ones that have no ability to escape or are willingly contained such as with 507.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
therefore they can't be safe.

There are over 30 Safe class humanoids according to a tag search.

item.title: Re: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded: Most humanoids are Euclid because they require some sort of care to contain. So therefore they can't be safe.

item.title: My first LOL at an SCP
item.content:encoded:
I actually laughed at that last one. I was expecting a slowly building explanation for its Euclid classification but instead got an image of spiders forming 200…

That does remind me though. Why is it Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just about sums my reaction to this guy. O)_(O

item.title: MOAR SPIDERS
item.content:encoded:
Sooo, tagged this as 'collaboration' because this seems like a fun thing for people to throw their hats into. Anyone is free to add testing logs with these guys following the provided format. There are but two rules that must be adhered to:

1. New entries should go before the final test here, I like it as an endpoint.

2. Entries must use D-11424 as the test subject.

3. (surprise! I lied about there only being two.) They should be entertaining. I'll cull entries that aren't up to snuff.

Have fun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. I like the idea here and the last test log was chuckle worthy, but other than that this didn't really do anything for me. Novoting for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should make this the title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spider Mime, Spider Mime,
Full of spiders all the time!

Just these two lines by themselves are the funniest thing I have ever seen. XD The Foundation is trying to kill me with laughter tonight!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really wish I could upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spider Mime, Spider Mime,
Full of spiders all the time!
Anomalous, intangible,
Spiders make him terrible
Hey hey, contain the Spider Mime!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hrm. I did have a ton of squick from the old article I already left out, and the remainder isn't doing much for the piece here. I cut the body horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really surprised that this is two independent articles fused together, because the concepts don't really click for me. While I like the spiders, I felt like they were just thrown in for the ick factor of parasitic spiders, if that makes sense. This is a no vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bwahahaha!!

Yea, I find I do characters best when I just subject them to the worst shit I can. No punishment is fitting enough though, indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahhh… ghost mime sentenced to being eternally frustrated by spiders. Somewhat less than the punishment I'd prefer visited on the beast for the crime of miming, but we must take justice where we can find it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mime fulla spiders?

…Mime fulla spiders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3247 will gather here, feeding on the substance as it is produced

Probably should be -A

+1, though I really want to know how the spiders and the mime came to be connected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hrm. I can't help but love the idea of these things being a nuisance to the mime, it's a weird kind of hilarity I'm not sure I can part with.

Perhaps I could do a little something to bring this aspect out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda agree; I had read both of the deleted skips and the new spider-props probably work on their own without the mime.

Tentative upvote for now, though mostly because of the second test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Four dozen SCP-3247 paired off to form what seemed to be marijuana cigarettes.

wrong designation?

I kinda get where you're coming from for this but it feels like if you remove the mime, you get a tighter article. +1 anyway. Would be fun with pics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that poor mime can't mime without his dang spiders making props. a tragedy. +1

item.title: Re: A Magic Mime and his Mighty Morphin' Method Acting Spider Kids
item.content:encoded: More of a Frozenpost in this case as in its beyond cold

item.title: Re: A Magic Mime and his Mighty Morphin' Method Acting Spider Kids
item.content:encoded:
does this count as a coldpost?

A Frankenpost maybe?

item.title: A Magic Mime and his Mighty Morphin' Method Acting Spider Kids
item.content:encoded:
So, a while back I deleted a few of my SCPs that I wasn't particularly fond of. I decided to take two of them; the former "Mimetic Entity", an SCP about an intangible mime, and "Motherhood" an SCP about a bunch of parasite spiders, and blended them into this unholy amalgamation.

If I got critique on the old SCPs… does this count as a coldpost?
